---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 8b8ec29942393c9893f75249a771c01b0d68c834
findings:
  - { id: edge-dc-3-exempts-spec--workbench-directory-dc-4, kind: computed, text: "decision dc-3 exempts spec/workbench-directory: resolved (EXEMPT)", disposition: exempt, note: "the deleted signal for a MANAGED WORKTREE is checked local-ref-only, narrower than the general store-layout gc deleted-definition dc-4 invokes by reference, because a worktree is bound to one specific local branch (this story's own dc-1) and cannot be resurrected from a surviving remote-tracking ref without an explicit act dc-1 already forbids; dc-4's general reclamation model (merged-or-deleted, dirty-is-kept) stays valid and unweakened, this story's worktree-specific implementation of the deleted leg is excused from the broader multi-target reading", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
  - { id: edge-dc-4-exempts-spec--workbench-directory-dc-4, kind: computed, text: "decision dc-4 exempts spec/workbench-directory: resolved (EXEMPT)", disposition: exempt, note: "this story adds a keep-and-disclose reason (a live-locked worktree, dc-2) beyond dc-4's one named exception (uncommitted changes); excused because the lock hold is bounded to a single git-worktree-mutating operation - a transient, self-resolving race window, never an indefinite hold - so dc-4's reclamation model stays valid and unweakened, this story adds a narrow safety margin around it rather than a competing kept-forever category", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
  - { id: judged-wtm-retention-edge-routing, kind: judged, text: "dc-3's second divergence (retention_days does not apply to managed worktrees; reclaim is immediate with no grace period) is declared edge-less on the ground that verdi-store-layout is a component spec that cannot be an exempts target. But the story's own dc-3 already demonstrates the available routing for exactly this shape of divergence: parent feature dc-4 invokes 'the ratified gc signals' generically, and the deleted-signal narrowing — likewise a divergence from store-layout's ratified gc semantics reached through that same generic invocation — carries a declared exempts edge against parent dc-4. If store-layout's 'gc horizon for merged/deleted refs' comment is read as part of the ratified gc semantics parent dc-4 incorporates, then reclaiming a worktree the instant its signal fires diverges from parent dc-4 through the identical channel, and parent dc-4 is a legal exempts target (a feature-decision fragment). The 'no edge available' claim holds only against store-layout itself, not against the parent decision that generically imports it — leaving the retention divergence undeclared against spec/workbench-directory dc-4 while its twin (the deleted signal) is declared. Mitigating reading: parent dc-4's text enumerates only the two signals ('merged ... or deleted (absent) is reclaimable') and never mentions a horizon, so retention_days may be outside what dc-4 ratified, making the immediate-reclaim choice consistent with the parent and divergent only from the un-targetable component spec — which is presumably the story's implicit position, but it is nowhere stated, whereas the deleted-signal case argues its routing explicitly. (confidence 0.45)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/workbench-directory#dc-1, spec/workbench-directory#dc-2, spec/workbench-directory#dc-3, spec/workbench-directory#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-5, spec/worktree-manager#dc-1, spec/worktree-manager#dc-2, spec/worktree-manager#dc-3, spec/worktree-manager#dc-4, spec/worktree-manager#dc-5] }

integrity: sha256:007acfe256c530c4e2f6421825e0ac9d821a879c9e1449d600be25f14c523b2a
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"wtm-retention-edge-routing\",\"text\":\"dc-3's second divergence (retention_days does not apply to managed worktrees; reclaim is immediate with no grace period) is declared edge-less on the ground that verdi-store-layout is a component spec that cannot be an exempts target. But the story's own dc-3 already demonstrates the available routing for exactly this shape of divergence: parent feature dc-4 invokes 'the ratified gc signals' generically, and the deleted-signal narrowing — likewise a divergence from store-layout's ratified gc semantics reached through that same generic invocation — carries a declared exempts edge against parent dc-4. If store-layout's 'gc horizon for merged/deleted refs' comment is read as part of the ratified gc semantics parent dc-4 incorporates, then reclaiming a worktree the instant its signal fires diverges from parent dc-4 through the identical channel, and parent dc-4 is a legal exempts target (a feature-decision fragment). The 'no edge available' claim holds only against store-layout itself, not against the parent decision that generically imports it — leaving the retention divergence undeclared against spec/workbench-directory dc-4 while its twin (the deleted signal) is declared. Mitigating reading: parent dc-4's text enumerates only the two signals ('merged ... or deleted (absent) is reclaimable') and never mentions a horizon, so retention_days may be outside what dc-4 ratified, making the immediate-reclaim choice consistent with the parent and divergent only from the un-targetable component spec — which is presumably the story's implicit position, but it is nowhere stated, whereas the deleted-signal case argues its routing explicitly.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

- **edge-dc-3-exempts-spec--workbench-directory-dc-4** [exempt]: decision dc-3 exempts spec/workbench-directory: resolved (EXEMPT) — the deleted signal for a MANAGED WORKTREE is checked local-ref-only, narrower than the general store-layout gc deleted-definition dc-4 invokes by reference, because a worktree is bound to one specific local branch (this story's own dc-1) and cannot be resurrected from a surviving remote-tracking ref without an explicit act dc-1 already forbids; dc-4's general reclamation model (merged-or-deleted, dirty-is-kept) stays valid and unweakened, this story's worktree-specific implementation of the deleted leg is excused from the broader multi-target reading
- **edge-dc-4-exempts-spec--workbench-directory-dc-4** [exempt]: decision dc-4 exempts spec/workbench-directory: resolved (EXEMPT) — this story adds a keep-and-disclose reason (a live-locked worktree, dc-2) beyond dc-4's one named exception (uncommitted changes); excused because the lock hold is bounded to a single git-worktree-mutating operation - a transient, self-resolving race window, never an indefinite hold - so dc-4's reclamation model stays valid and unweakened, this story adds a narrow safety margin around it rather than a competing kept-forever category

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-wtm-retention-edge-routing** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: dc-3's second divergence (retention_days does not apply to managed worktrees; reclaim is immediate with no grace period) is declared edge-less on the ground that verdi-store-layout is a component spec that cannot be an exempts target. But the story's own dc-3 already demonstrates the available routing for exactly this shape of divergence: parent feature dc-4 invokes 'the ratified gc signals' generically, and the deleted-signal narrowing — likewise a divergence from store-layout's ratified gc semantics reached through that same generic invocation — carries a declared exempts edge against parent dc-4. If store-layout's 'gc horizon for merged/deleted refs' comment is read as part of the ratified gc semantics parent dc-4 incorporates, then reclaiming a worktree the instant its signal fires diverges from parent dc-4 through the identical channel, and parent dc-4 is a legal exempts target (a feature-decision fragment). The 'no edge available' claim holds only against store-layout itself, not against the parent decision that generically imports it — leaving the retention divergence undeclared against spec/workbench-directory dc-4 while its twin (the deleted signal) is declared. Mitigating reading: parent dc-4's text enumerates only the two signals ('merged ... or deleted (absent) is reclaimable') and never mentions a horizon, so retention_days may be outside what dc-4 ratified, making the immediate-reclaim choice consistent with the parent and divergent only from the un-targetable component spec — which is presumably the story's implicit position, but it is nowhere stated, whereas the deleted-signal case argues its routing explicitly. (confidence 0.45)
